title: Connecting polygenic disease risk to cell states and regulatory programs through single-cell chromatin accessibility
authors: Yu L, Deary LT, Liu Q, Zhang Q, Zhao S
abstract: Single-cell ATAC-sequencing enables high-resolution mapping of cell states and their putative regulatory elements. The enrichment of GWAS signals within context-specific regulatory regions can reveal disease-relevant cell populations. SCADS (Single-Cell ATAC-seq Disease Score) integrates GWAS summary statistics with scATAC-seq data to prioritize disease-relevant cells: (i) identifying chromatin co-regulatory regions via topic modeling, (ii) quantifying their polygenic GWAS enrichment, and (iii) calculating cell-specific disease scores. Applied to autoimmune traits including IBD; framework generalizes to noncoding regulatory variation across traits.
